items.correct: Removing material from a solid block to reveal the form (e.g., carving stone).
items.why: Students confuse 'subtractive' with arithmetic. In sculpture, additive = building up (clay, welding); subtractive = taking away (carving). Michelangelo described 'freeing' figures from the marble - quintessential subtractive thinking.

items.q: What is the significance of Duchamp's 'Fountain'?
items.correct: It challenged the definition of art - the artist's choice, not their skill, becomes the artwork.
items.random_wrong: It is a study of plumbing.
items.why: Students miss the conceptual point. Duchamp didn't make the urinal - he selected and presented it. The radical claim is that ART IS DESIGNATION, opening the door to all conceptual art.

items.correct: The small projecting line at the end of letter strokes.
items.misconception: A whole typeface that looks formal.
items.why: Students conflate the FEATURE (the small foot/stroke) with the CLASSIFICATION (serif typefaces, which have them). Sans serif means 'without serifs' - those little projections at letter endings.

items.q: Carving is which type of process?
items.why: Correct answer: Subtractive. “Additive” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Reclining figures. “Pop art prints” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Year of Tracey Emin's 'My Bed' installation?
items.why: Exact answer: 1998. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
